message.content:
Search the codebase at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster for how GAR (Google Artifact Registry) image tags are composed. Look for:
1. How the full GAR image reference is built (region + host + project + repo + image tag)
2. How kludge images are tagged differently from summoned images
3. What the "hallmark" identifier looks like in image tags
4. Look in Tools/rbk/rbfr_cli.sh (rbfr_summon) and Tools/rbk/rbfd_cli.sh (rbfd_kludge) for the image reference patterns

Also check the nameplate rbrn.env files under .rbk/ to see what fields a nameplate contains.

53	rbfr_wrest() {
54	  zrbfr_sentinel
55	
56	  local z_locator="${1:-}"
57	
58	  # Documentation block
59	  buc_doc_brief "Wrest an image from the registry to local container runtime by locator"
60	  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in moniker:tag format"
61	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
62	
63	  # Validate locator parameter
64	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (moniker:tag)"
65	
66	  # Parse locator into moniker and tag
67	  case "${z_locator}" in
68	    *:*) : ;;
69	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected moniker:tag" ;;
70	  esac
71	  local z_moniker="${z_locator%%:*}"
72	  local z_tag="${z_locator#*:}"
73	  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "Moniker is empty in locator"
74	  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
75	
76	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
77	
78	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
79	
80	  # Get OAuth token
81	  local z_token
82	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
83	
84	  buc_step "Logging into container registry"
85	
86	  # Construct full image reference
87	  local z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${z_locator}"
88	
89	  # Docker login to GAR
90	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
91	    || buc_die "Container runtime authentication failed"
92	
93	  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"
94	
95	  # Pull image
96	  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"
97	
98	  # Get local image ID
99	  local z_image_id
100	  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
101	    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
102	  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")
103	
104	  # Display results
105	  echo ""
106	  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
107	  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"
108	
109	  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
110	}

112	rbfr_summon() {
113	  zrbfr_sentinel
114	
115	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
116	  z_vessel="${z_vessel##*/}"  # strip path prefix — accept directory path or bare moniker
117	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
118	
119	  # Documentation block
120	  buc_doc_brief "Summon an ark (pull -image, -about, and -vouch artifacts as a coherent unit)"
121	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel name (e.g., rbev-busybox)"
122	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
123	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

126	  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel parameter required"
127	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"
128

131	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
132	
133	  # Get OAuth token
134	  local z_token
135	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
136	
137	  # Construct ark tags — all use full hallmark
138	  local z_image_tag="${z_hallmark}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
139	  local z_about_tag="${z_hallmark}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_ABOUT}"
140	  local z_vouch_tag="${z_hallmark}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
141	
142	  buc_step "Verifying ark existence"
143	
144	  # Check if -image artifact exists
145	  local z_image_status_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_image_status.txt"
146	  local z_image_response_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_image_response.json"
147	
148	  curl --head -s                                     \
149	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
150	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"           \
151	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"           \
152	    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}"     \
153	    -w "%{http_code}"                               \
154	    -o "${z_image_response_file}"                   \
155	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_vessel}/manifests/${z_image_tag}" \
156	    > "${z_image_status_file}" || buc_die "HEAD request failed for -image artifact"
157	
158	  local z_image_http_code
159	  z_image_http_code=$(<"${z_image_status_file}")
160	  test -n "${z_image_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for -image"
161	
162	  local z_image_exists=false
163	  if test "${z_image_http_code}" = "200"; then
164	    z_image_exists=true
165	  elif test "${z_image_http_code}" != "404"; then
166	    buc_die "Unexpected HTTP status ${z_image_http_code} when checking -image artifact"
167	  fi
168	
169	  # Check if -about artifact exists
170	  local z_about_status_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_about_status.txt"
171	  local z_about_response_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_about_response.json"
172	
173	  curl --head -s                                     \
174	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
175	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"           \
176	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"           \
177	    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}"     \
178	    -w "%{http_code}"                               \
179	    -o "${z_about_response_file}"                   \
180	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_vessel}/manifests/${z_about_tag}" \
181	    > "${z_about_status_file}" || buc_die "HEAD request failed for -about artifact"
182	
183	  local z_about_http_code
184	  z_about_http_code=$(<"${z_about_status_file}")
185	  test -n "${z_about_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for -about"
186	
187	  local z_about_exists=false
188	  if test "${z_about_http_code}" = "200"; then
189	    z_about_exists=true
190	  elif test "${z_about_http_code}" != "404"; then
191	    buc_die "Unexpected HTTP status ${z_about_http_code} when checking -about artifact"
192	  fi
193	
194	  # Check if -vouch artifact exists
195	  local z_vouch_status_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_vouch_status.txt"
196	  local z_vouch_response_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_vouch_response.json"
197	
198	  curl --head -s                                     \
199	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
200	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"           \
201	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"           \
202	    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}"     \
203	    -w "%{http_code}"                               \
204	    -o "${z_vouch_response_file}"                   \
205	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_vessel}/manifests/${z_vouch_tag}" \

207

210	  test -n "${z_vouch_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for -vouch"
211	
212	  local z_vouch_exists=false
213	  if test "${z_vouch_http_code}" = "200"; then
214	    z_vouch_exists=true
215	  elif test "${z_vouch_http_code}" != "404"; then
216	    buc_die "Unexpected HTTP status ${z_vouch_http_code} when checking -vouch artifact"
217	  fi
218	
219	  # Evaluate ark state
220	  if test "${z_image_exists}" = "false" && test "${z_about_exists}" = "false"; then
221	    buc_die "Hallmark not found: neither -image nor -about exists"
222	  fi
223	
224	  if test "${z_image_exists}" = "true" && test "${z_about_exists}" = "false"; then
225	    buc_warn "Orphaned artifact detected: -image exists but -about is missing"
226	  elif test "${z_image_exists}" = "false" && test "${z_about_exists}" = "true"; then

101	# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
102	# Args: token mode
103	#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
104	zrbfd_quota_preflight() {
105	  zrbfd_sentinel
106	
107	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
108	
109	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_quota_preflight: token required"
110	
111	  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
112	  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
113	  case "${z_vcpus}" in
114	    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
115	      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
116	      return 0
117	      ;;
118	  esac
119	
120	  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"
121	
122	  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
123	  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
124	  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"
125	
126	  buc_step "Checking concurrent build quota"
127	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"
128	
129	  local z_code=""
130	  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
131	  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
132	    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
133	    return 0
134	  fi
135	
136	  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
137	  rbgu_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
138	    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRR_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
139	    || true
140	
141	  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
142	  local z_limit=""
143	  z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
144	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
145	    z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
146	      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
147	  fi
148	
149	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
150	    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
151	    return 0
152	  fi
153	
154	  # -1 means unlimited
155	  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
156	    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
157	    return 0
158	  fi
159	
160	  # Compute max concurrent builds
161	  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))
162	
163	  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"
164	
165	  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
166	    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
167	    buc_warn "Fresh depots start with a low quota. After some build activity, the Edit Quotas option becomes available."
168	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
169	  else
170	    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
171	  fi
172	}

174	# Internal: Verify that all required images exist in GAR before submitting
175	# to Cloud Build. Checks two layers in dependency order:
176	#
177	#   1. Reliquary — co-versioned builder tool images (gcloud, docker, syft, etc.)
178	#      created by inscribe. One reliquary per depot setup. Enshrine depends on it.
179	#   2. Enshrine — upstream base images copied into private GAR, pinned by content
180	#      hash. One enshrine per base image anchor. Multiple vessels sharing the same
181	#      anchor only need one enshrine.
182	#
183	# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
184	# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
185	#
186	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
187	# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
188	zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
189	  zrbfd_sentinel
190	
191	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
192	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
193	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
194	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"
195	
196	  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
197	  # Inscribe creates all 6 tool images atomically in one GCB job, so checking
198	  # one (docker:latest) is sufficient as a canary for the entire reliquary.
199	
200	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
201	  if test -n "${z_reliquary}"; then
202	    buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"
203	
204	    local -r z_rqy_canary="${z_reliquary}/docker"
205	    local -r z_rqy_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_status.txt"
206	    local -r z_rqy_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_response.txt"
207	    local -r z_rqy_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_stderr.txt"
208	
209	    curl --head -sS \
210	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
211	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
212	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
213	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
214	      -w "%{http_code}" \
215	      -o "${z_rqy_response_file}" \
216	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_rqy_canary}/manifests/latest" \
217	      > "${z_rqy_status_file}" 2>"${z_rqy_stderr_file}" \
218	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary canary: ${z_rqy_canary}:latest — see ${z_rqy_stderr_file}"
219	
220	    local z_rqy_http_code=""
221	    z_rqy_http_code=$(<"${z_rqy_status_file}")
222	    test -n "${z_rqy_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check"
223	
224	    if test "${z_rqy_http_code}" = "404"; then
225	      buc_warn "Reliquary not found: ${z_reliquary}"
226	      buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
227	      buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
228	      buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
229	      buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. All vessels in a depot"
230	      buc_bare "  typically share one reliquary. Inscribe creates a new datestamped set:"
231	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
232	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
233	      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — reliquary missing from GAR"
234	    elif test "${z_rqy_http_code}" != "200"; then
235	      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_rqy_http_code} when checking reliquary: ${z_rqy_canary}:latest"
236	    fi
237	
238	    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary}"
239	  fi
240	
241	  # --- Layer 2: Enshrined base images ---
242	  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
243	  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (content-addressed GAR tag). Enshrine copies the upstream
244	  # image into the enshrine namespace, pinned by the anchor hash. The conjure
245	  # Dockerfile's FROM references the anchor, not the upstream tag.
246	
247	  buc_step "Verifying enshrined base images exist in GAR"
248	
249	  local z_n=""
250	  local z_anchor_var=""
251	  local z_anchor=""
252	  local z_origin_var=""
253	  local z_origin=""
254	  local z_any_checked="false"
255	  local z_status_file=""
256	  local z_response_file=""
257	  local z_stderr_file=""
258	  local z_http_code=""
259	
260	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
261	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
262	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
263	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
264	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
265	
266	    # Skip slots without an origin or without an anchor (pass-through images don't need enshrine)
267	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
268	    test -n "${z_anchor}" || continue
269	
270	    z_any_checked="true"
271	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_status.txt"
272	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_response.txt"
273	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_stderr.txt"
274	
275	    curl --head -sS \
276	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
277	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
278	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
279	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
280	      -w "%{http_code}" \
281	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
282	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/enshrine/manifests/${z_anchor}" \
283	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
284	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for enshrined image: enshrine:${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
285	
286	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
287	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for enshrine check"
288	
289	    if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
290	      buc_warn "Enshrined base image not found: enshrine:${z_anchor} (from ${z_origin})"
291	      buc_bare "  Enshrine copies upstream base images (e.g., busybox:latest from Docker Hub) into"
292	      buc_bare "  your private GAR, pinned by content hash. Like the reliquary, this ensures"
293	      buc_bare "  air-gapped builds never reach the public internet. The anchor tag is stable"
294	      buc_bare "  until you deliberately re-enshrine to pick up a newer upstream version."
295	      buc_bare "  Multiple vessels sharing the same base image anchor need only one enshrine."
296	      buc_bare "  Run enshrine, then re-run ordain:"
297	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
298	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
299	      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — enshrined base image missing from GAR"
300	    elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
301	      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking enshrined image: enshrine:${z_anchor}"
302	    fi
303	
304	    buc_log_args "Enshrined image verified: enshrine:${z_anchor}"
305	  done
306	
307	  if test "${z_any_checked}" = "true"; then
308	    buc_info "All enshrined base images verified in GAR"
309	  fi
310	}

313	zrbfd_stitch_build_json() {
314	  zrbfd_sentinel
315	
316	  local -r z_output_path="${1:?Output path required}"
317	  local -r z_inscribe_ts="${2:?Inscribe timestamp required}"
318	  local -r z_context_tag="${3:?Context image tag required}"
319	
320	  buc_log_args "Stitching builds.create JSON to ${z_output_path}"
321	
322	  # Preconditions: vessel loaded and git state captured
323	  test -s "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}" || buc_die "Vessel not loaded — call zrbfc_load_vessel first"
324	  test -s "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}"     || buc_die "Git info not captured — ensure git metadata is captured before stitch"
325	
326	  buc_log_args 'Read vessel state for substitutions'
327	  local -r z_sigil=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}")
328	  test -n "${z_sigil}" || buc_die "Empty vessel sigil"
329	  local -r z_dockerfile_name="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE##*/}"
330	  local -r z_platforms="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}"
331	
332	  # Resolve base images: ANCHOR → full GAR reference, or pass ORIGIN through
333	  # Spec: RBSAC step "Resolve Base Images"
334	  local -r z_gar_image_prefix="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/enshrine"
335	  local z_image_ref_1="" z_image_ref_2="" z_image_ref_3=""
336	  local z_ri_n="" z_ri_origin_var="" z_ri_anchor_var="" z_ri_origin="" z_ri_anchor=""
337	  for z_ri_n in 1 2 3; do
338	    z_ri_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ORIGIN"
339	    z_ri_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ANCHOR"
340	    z_ri_origin="${!z_ri_origin_var:-}"
341	    z_ri_anchor="${!z_ri_anchor_var:-}"
342	    test -n "${z_ri_origin}" || continue

106	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
107	    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"
108	
109	  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${z_reliquary}"
110	  ZRBFC_TOOL_GCLOUD="${z_rqy_prefix}/gcloud:latest"
111	  ZRBFC_TOOL_DOCKER="${z_rqy_prefix}/docker:latest"
112	  ZRBFC_TOOL_ALPINE="${z_rqy_prefix}/alpine:latest"
113	  ZRBFC_TOOL_SYFT="${z_rqy_prefix}/syft:latest"
114	  ZRBFC_TOOL_BINFMT="${z_rqy_prefix}/binfmt:latest"
115	  ZRBFC_TOOL_SKOPEO="${z_rqy_prefix}/skopeo:latest"
116	  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
117	}
118	
119	# Resolve vessel argument: accepts a sigil (e.g., rbev-sentry-debian-slim) or a path
120	# (e.g., rbev-vessels/rbev-sentry-debian-slim).  On no-arg or invalid arg, lists
121	# available vessels and dies.  On success, writes resolved path to ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE.
122	zrbfc_resolve_vessel() {
123	  zrbfc_sentinel
124	
125	  local -r z_arg="${1:-}"
126	
127	  # Try as path first, then as sigil under RBRR_VESSEL_DIR
128	  if test -n "${z_arg}" && test -d "${z_arg}" && test -f "${z_arg}/rbrv.env"; then
129	    printf '%s' "${z_arg}" > "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}" \
130	      || buc_die "Failed to write resolved vessel path"
131	    return 0
132	  fi
133	  if test -n "${z_arg}" && test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}" && test -f "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}/rbrv.env"; then
134	    printf '%s' "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_arg}" > "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}" \
135	      || buc_die "Failed to write resolved vessel path"
136	    return 0
137	  fi
138	
139	  # Resolution failed — list available vessels and die
140	  local z_sigils=""
141	  z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
142	  buc_step "Available vessels:"
143	  local z_sigil=""
144	  for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
145	    buc_bare "        ${z_sigil}"
146	  done
147	  if test -z "${z_arg}"; then
148	    buc_die "Vessel argument required (sigil or path)"
149	  fi
150	  buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_arg}"

27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbrn_*)
29	
30	zrbrn_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn already kindled"
32	
33	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
34	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
35	
36	  # Enroll all RBRN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
37	
38	  buv_regime_enroll RBRN
39	
40	  buv_group_enroll "Core Service Identity"
41	  buv_xname_enroll   RBRN_MONIKER                 2   12  "Unique identifier for Crucible"
42	  buv_string_enroll  RBRN_DESCRIPTION              0  120  "Human-readable description"
43	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_RUNTIME                              "Container runtime: docker or podman" \
44	                     docker podman
45	
46	  buv_group_enroll "Container Image Configuration"
47	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Sentry Image"
48	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
49	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Sentry Image"
50	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Bottle Image"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Entry Service Configuration"
53	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE                           "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled" \
54	                     disabled enabled
55	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE  enabled
56	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION               "External port on Transit Network"
57	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE                   "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"
58	
59	  buv_group_enroll "Enclave Network Configuration"
60	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP                      "Base IPv4 for enclave network"
61	  buv_decimal_enroll RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK          8   30  "Network mask width (8-30)"
62	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP                    "IP address for Sentry Container"
63	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP                    "IP address for Bottle Container"
64	
65	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Core"
66	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN                      "Minimum port for outbound connections"
67	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE                      "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
68	                     disabled global allowlist
69	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE                   "IP access mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
70	                     disabled global allowlist
71	
72	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink DNS Allowlist"
73	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE  allowlist
74	  buv_list_domain_enroll RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS           "Allowed DNS domains"
75	
76	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Access Allowlist"
77	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  allowlist
78	  buv_list_cidr_enroll   RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS             "Allowed CIDR ranges"
79	
80	  # Guard against unexpected RBRN_ variables not in enrollment
81	  buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_
82

84	  buv_lock RBRN

89	zrbrn_sentinel() {
90	  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
91	}

93	# Enforce all RBRN enrollment validations and custom format checks
94	zrbrn_enforce() {
95	  zrbrn_sentinel
96	
97	  buv_vet RBRN
98	
99	  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
100	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
101	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
102	
103	  # Cross-port check (entry ports must be less than uplink port min)
104	  if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
105	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
106	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
107	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
108	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
109	  fi
110	
111	  # Build docker env args array from validated values
112	  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
113	  buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
114	}

116	######################################################################
117	# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
118	
119	# List available nameplate monikers as space-separated tokens
120	# Prerequisite: RBCC sourced (needs RBBC_dot_dir, RBCC_rbrn_file)
121	rbrn_list_capture() {
122	  zrbcc_sentinel
123	
124	  local z_result=""
125	  local z_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
126	  local z_i=""
127	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
128	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
129	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
130	    local z_moniker="${z_dir##*/}"
131	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_moniker}"
132	  done
133	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
134	  echo "${z_result}"
135	}
136	
137	######################################################################
138	# Cross-Nameplate Functions
139	#
140	# rbrn_preflight:  Requires RBCC kindled (called from CLI rbrn_audit)
141	
142	# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
143	zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
144	  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
145	  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
146	  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
147	}
148	
149	# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
150	# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
151	zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
152	  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
153	  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
154	  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
155	  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
156	  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then

183	  local z_ws_port_keys=()

185	  local z_enc_port_keys=()
186	  local z_enc_port_vals=()

247	    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
248	    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))

254	        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
255	      fi

## Summary: GAR Image Tag Composition in rbm_alpha_recipemuster

Based on my search of the codebase, here's how GAR image tags are composed and used:

### 1. **Full GAR Image Reference Structure**

The complete GAR image reference is built from four components:

```
{ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/{ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/{vessel}:{tag}
```

Where:
- **ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST**: `${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}`
  - `RBGD_GAR_LOCATION`: GCP region (e.g., `us-central1`)
  - `RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX`: `-docker.pkg.dev` (constant)
  - **Result**: `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev`

- **ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH**: `${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}`
  - Example: `my-project/recipe-bottle`

- **vessel**: The vessel sigil (e.g., `rbev-bottle-ifrit`)

- **tag**: The image tag composed of hallmark + suffix

**Complete example**: `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/recipe-bottle/rbev-bottle-ifrit:c260404125932-r260404200037-image`

### 2. **How Kludge Images are Tagged (Differently from Summoned Images)**

**Kludge images** (local development builds) use a distinct hallmark prefix:

```bash
# File: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, line 1233
z_hallmark="k${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"
```

- **Prefix**: `k` (for "kludge")
- **Format**: `kYYMMDDHHMMSS-{git_context}`
- **Example**: `k260404201512-abc123def` (where `260404201512` is timestamp and `abc123def` is git commit)
- **Key difference**: Kludge prefix is `k` to distinguish from conjure (`c`) or bind (`b`) modes

**Summoned images** (from GAR via retrieval) use the stored hallmark which has different prefixes:
- **Conjure**: `c` prefix (e.g., `c260404125932-r260404200037`)
- **Bind**: `b` prefix (e.g., `b260331155120-r260331225120`)

### 3. **The "Hallmark" Identifier Structure**

Hallmarks follow the pattern: `{prefix}{timestamp1}-{timestamp2}`

**Components**:
- **Prefix** (1 char):
  - `c` = conjure (cloud build)
  - `b` = bind (mirrored upstream image)
  - `k` = kludge (local development build)
  
- **First timestamp** (12 chars): `YYMMDDHHMMSS` (creation time)
- **Hyphen separator**: `-`
- **Second timestamp** (12 chars): `YYMMDDHHMMSS` (for conjure/bind, build/registry timestamp)

**Examples from nameplate files**:
- Sentry hallmark: `c260331154527-r260331224806` (conjure, built 26-03-31 15:45:27, registered 26-03-31 22:48:06)
- Bottle hallmark: `b260331155120-r260331225120` (bind/mirror, built 26-03-31 15:51:20, registered 26-03-31 22:51:20)

### 4. **Ark Artifact Suffixes**

All hallmarks are combined with artifact type suffixes (defined in rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 122-126):

```bash
RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE="-image"    # Container image
RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_ABOUT="-about"    # Metadata about the image
RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH="-vouch"    # Attestation/proof artifact
RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_DIAGS="-diags"    # Diagnostic information
```

Complete tag examples:
- `c260331154527-r260331224806-image` (actual container image)
- `c260331154527-r260331224806-about` (metadata)
- `c260331154527-r260331224806-vouch` (attestation)

### 5. **Nameplate Configuration Fields**

Nameplate files (rbrn.env) contain these key fields:

**File locations**: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/.rbk/{nameplate_name}/rbrn.env`

**Core fields**:
- `RBRN_MONIKER`: Unique identifier (2-12 chars, alphanumeric + hyphen)
- `RBRN_DESCRIPTION`: Human description
- `RBRN_RUNTIME`: Container runtime (docker or podman)

**Container image configuration**:
- `RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL`: Vessel identifier for sentry image (e.g., `rbev-sentry-debian-slim`)
- `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL`: Vessel identifier for bottle image (e.g., `rbev-bottle-ifrit`)
- `RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK`: Full hallmark for sentry (e.g., `c260331154527-r260331224806`)
- `RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK`: Full hallmark for bottle (e.g., `c260404125932-r260404200037`)

**Port configuration**:
- `RBRN_ENTRY_MODE`: enabled/disabled
- `RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION`: External port
- `RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE`: Internal port

**Network configuration**:
- `RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP`: Network base (e.g., `10.242.0.0`)
- `RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK`: Netmask width (8-30)
- `RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP`: Sentry container IP
- `RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP`: Bottle container IP

**Uplink configuration**:
- `RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN`: Minimum outbound port
- `RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE`: disabled/global/allowlist
- `RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE`: disabled/global/allowlist
- `RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS`: CSV list of allowed domains
- `RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS`: CSV list of allowed CIDR ranges

- `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` - Artifact suffixes (lines 122-126)
- `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbrn_regime.sh` - Nameplate validation
